title: MCP Agent
summary:
# 1. MCP 协议概述

## 1.1 MCP 的定义、目标和设计理念

### 什么是 MCP 协议

**Model Context Protocol (MCP)** 是由 Anthropic 设计的开放协议，专门用于连接 AI 助手与外部数据源和工具。MCP 的核心目标是为 AI Agent 提供一个标准化、安全且可扩展的方式来访问外部资源。

### 设计理念

MCP 协议遵循以下核心设计理念：

1. **标准化接口**：定义统一的消息格式和交互模式
1. **安全第一**：内置权限控制和数据隔离机制
1. **可扩展性**：支持自定义资源类型和工具扩展
1. **简单易用**

title: [PR] fix(query-guard): exclude human-interaction wait from session timeout
summary:
Fixes #1878

## What changed and why

The session query watchdog (`QueryGuard`) counts the time a query spends blocked on a user decision toward its idle timeout (5 min). While a query is legitimately waiting on a permission prompt, `AskUserQuestion`, or plan-mode selection, no lease is held, so aft

title: [PR] perf(boot): don't boot a throwaway runtime before onboarding
summary:
## Problem

On a genuinely-fresh install, the server-only boot (`startEliza`, `packages/app-core/src/runtime/eliza.ts`) bound the API and then **always booted a full default agent runtime** — PGlite open (~503ms), migrations, plugin-sql registration, default character build, services — even though t

title: [PR] feat(rag): parse PDF links via web fetch and crawling (AYC-266)
summary:
## AYC-266 — Support PDF parsing via web fetch

Fetching a URL that points to a PDF previously failed: `CheerioUrlRetrieverHandler` rejected any non-HTML/text response with `UnsupportedContentTypeError`. As a result the `website_content` tool errored on PDF links and the knowledge-base crawler silen

title: [PR] fix(command-semantics): treat linter exit 1 as violations, not an error
summary:
## Summary

- Add lint-aware exit-code semantics for `ruff` and `eslint` in both the Bash and PowerShell `commandSemantics` tables: exit 1 = "violations found" (not an error), 2+ = a real error — mirroring the existing `grep` / `diff` pattern.
- `uvx` / `npx` wrapper invocations inherit the wrapped

title: [PR] fix(config): recover from a healthy backup when the global config is corrupt
summary:
## Summary

A present-but-corrupt `~/.openclaude.json` takes `getConfig` down the `ConfigParseError` path, which either resets to defaults (silently discarding the user's settings) or re-throws — even though healthy timestamped backups exist in `~/.claude/backups`. Previously `getConfig` only consul

title: [PR] fix(provider): add Use Anthropic option to switch back from third-party profiles
summary:
Fixes #1426

## Problem

Once any third-party provider profile is active, `/provider` offers no way back to built-in Anthropic. `getActiveProviderProfile` falls back to `profiles[0]` whenever `activeProviderProfileId` is unset, so clearing the active id still re-selects a saved profile. The only esc

Adds Cloudflare Workers AI as a first-class OpenAI-compatible provider preset, mirroring the Venice (#1109) / Xiaomi MiMo (#1152) descriptor pattern.

- New `src/integrations/vendors/cloudflare.ts` descriptor:
  - `classification: 'openai-compatible'`
  - Default base URL with literal `<

title: [PR] Bound provider calls and reclaim expired leases in services gateway
summary:
Fixes #23

A hung litellm HTTP call never returns, so its gateway lease was held until the owning process died. With the machine-wide LLM cap of 2, two hung calls froze every skill in every process for the full 3600s wait timeout. Observed after switching `LLM_MODEL` to a cloud provider: a stalled

title: [PR] Fix code review semantic-exhaustion failures on reasoning models

title: [PR] Add deployable personal AI assistant system
summary:
## Summary

A self-hosted personal AI assistant, hardened over three review passes and then extended through a five-phase feature roadmap. Everything is free/open-source, sized to a Pi 5 / Pi 4 / on-demand GPU topology, and degrades gracefully when the GPU box is off. **243 tests, CI green.**

title: [PR] feat(plugins): let plugins ship command hooks in Infer's native hooks.yaml format
summary:
Resolves #771

## Summary

Let plugins ship command hooks in Infer's native hooks.yaml format. A plugin's
hooks.yaml is materialized at install time, validated against domain.HookPoints,
and listed in the install summary. Plugin hooks are disabled by default and
opt-in per plugin via a hooks_enabled
